prompt: I need a synchronous wrapper around an async client for legacy code. Right now it calls `asyncio.run()` inside methods, sometimes from AnyIO worker threads, sometimes from foreign threads, and sometimes while an app framework already has a running loop. One worker-thread callback only needs to set an AnyIO `Event`, and another long-running sync function should stop promptly when the host task is cancelled. Show the correct AnyIO-first boundary and explain when `BlockingPortal` is the right tool.
assertions: It says nested `asyncio.run()` under a running loop is invalid.
It distinguishes AnyIO worker threads from foreign threads.
It introduces `BlockingPortal` or token-based access for sync-to-async bridging.
It states that `from_thread.run()` / `from_thread.run_sync()` work directly only from AnyIO worker threads spawned via `to_thread.run_sync()`.
It distinguishes `from_thread.run()` for async callables from `from_thread.run_sync()` for loop-thread sync callbacks such as setting an AnyIO `Event`.
It mentions `current_token()` as the lighter one-off bridge for foreign threads.
It mentions `from_thread.check_cancelled()` for cooperative cancellation in long-running worker-thread code.
It does not recommend `BlockingPortal` for a one-off callback from an AnyIO worker thread when `from_thread.run()` or `from_thread.run_sync()` is enough.
It does not promise that cancelling the caller will stop arbitrary worker-thread code.
